free_text: Activation cross-sections on cadmium: Proton induced
nuclear reactions up to 80 MeV
Preliminary data.
Natural cadmium target foils of thickness 15.6 micron.
Four stacks were irradiated.
The primary energies of the beams: 33, 70, 70, 80 MeV,
the beam intensities: 146, 125, 124, 47 nA,
the irradiation times: 30, 30, 5.4, 71 min,
respectively.
Nondestructive measurement without chemical separation.
Stopping and energy degradation were calculated using
the Andersen-Ziegler polynomial approximation.
Decay data were taken from WWW Table of Radioactive
Isotopes by Ekstrom and Firestone, database version:
Information from Tarkanyi.
Thicknesses of Al foils: 476, 500, 1000 micron,
thicknesses of Cu foils: 7.6, 50, 54, 150 micron.
Chapter 4
Estimated combining the uncertainties of the
energy degradation depending on the thickness of the
target foils in the stack and energy straggling.
Error sources:
* number of the bombarding particles (7%),
* number of the target nuclei (3%),
* decay data (3%),
* detector efficiency (5%),
* peak area (1-10%).
The linearly contributing independent processes were
taken into account extracting square root of
quadratically summed relative uncertainties.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the model code ALICE-IPPE.
On the basis of the new experimental cross
sections and the results of the model calculations
integral yields were calculated and compared to
experimental yields taken from the literature.

partly via the 1529.7 keV independent but weak gamma
line partly separating the contribution of the ground
state to the common 632.97 keV gamma line.

the ground state.

of 105mAg (7.23 min).
